headline: Why France's national weather service struggles to accurately predict heatwaves
subtitle: Days ahead of the heatwaves of June and July, Météo-France slightly underestimated their intensity and duration. The reason has to do with limits in forecasting models for extreme events, as well as the challenge of explaining the uncertainty of weather predictions to the general public.

From Paris to Nice, Emmanuel Macron marks Bastille Day with a series of commemorations
subtitle: For his final national holiday as president, Emmanuel Macron presided over an unprecedented military parade and paid solemn tribute to the victims of the Nice terror attack.

headline: US targets ICC in new offensive
subtitle: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio reopened a diplomatic campaign on Monday aimed at dismantling the ICC by combining sanctions, putting pressure on member states and seeking allies among major powers hostile to the institution.

headline: China's foreign trade surges, driven by AI and electric vehicles
subtitle: China's exports rose by 27% in June compared to the same month last year. Despite an increase in imports, the country posted a trade surplus of $576 billion in the first half of the year.

headline: What does France's assisted dying bill contain, as MPs prepare to cast their final vote?
subtitle: As it reaches the end of its parliamentary journey with Wednesday's vote in the  Assemblée Nationale, the proposed law would establish the right to assisted suicide or euthanasia for patients experiencing constant suffering from serious and incurable illnesses, under strictly regulated conditions.

headline: Macron and Zelensky, a special relationship marked by misunderstandings
subtitle: Ukrainian soldiers will join France's annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday, July 14. French President Emmanuel Macron and his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelensky will stand together in a sign of Paris's support for Kyiv, but their relationship has not always been so straightforward.

headline: 'If they catch me, it's over for me': Senegal's LGBTQ+ community lives in daily fear of repression
subtitle: In March, Senegal passed a law increasing penalties for same-sex relations, leaving LGBTQ+ people living in fear. Several described their deeply uncertain daily lives, gripped by the constant anxiety of being arrested.
